rationale.R1.4: The text mentions prior work on calibration but doesn't clearly articulate how CalibRL differs from these works beyond a vague statement about using Brier score.
rationale.R1.7: While the text cites some relevant papers, it lacks specific details about missing or incorrect citations. It also doesn't discuss the process of identifying and correcting potential citation errors.
rationale.RX.1: The text discusses uncertainty in the context of calibration error but doesn't explicitly mark confidence levels for individual recommendations or identify missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The text focuses on a specific task (TriviaQA) and its out-of-distribution generalization, suggesting the scope is well-defined.
rationale.RX.4: No harmful content or regressions are mentioned in the text.
rationale.RX.5: The text doesn't provide details about how sources are traced or how non-cosmetic changes are annotated.
